ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for steamed tilapia; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared fish dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed tilapia is one fillet weighing approximately 150 g, providing around 204 kcal, 33 g protein, and 7.8 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient Composition of Foods — Steamed Tilapia (F03010037)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for steamed tilapia, record ID F03010037. All core macros and reported micronutrients are as published. Saturated fat (1.6 g/100 g) cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for tilapia (NDB 15262) for consistency validation; HPB primary values retained.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index Not Applicable — Negligible Carbohydrate Content
gi_source.source_match_notes: Steamed tilapia contains 0 g carbohydrates per 100 g. Per the University of Sydney GI Database methodology, glycemic index testing requires a minimum available carbohydrate content; foods with negligible carbohydrates are classified as not applicable. No GI value is assigned. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. This classification is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database approach for protein-dominant foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (85 mg/100 g) with high potassium (351 mg/100 g) yielding a favourable Na:K ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Steamed tilapia is appropriate for hypertensive individuals due to its low sodium content and high potassium level; however, patients should avoid adding soy sauce or salt at the table, as this can substantially increase sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar result in no glycaemic impact; high protein (22 g/100 g) supports satiety and postprandial blood glucose stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Steamed tilapia is an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as it contains no carbohydrates or sugar and will not raise blood glucose; patients should avoid sweet glazes or sauces that would add carbohydrates to this otherwise carbohydrate-free preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean white fish with moderate total fat (5.2 g/100 g), low saturated fat (1.6 g/100 g), and omega-3 fatty acids supporting cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Steamed tilapia is a heart-healthy protein choice with low saturated fat and no added oils from the steaming method; individuals with cardiovascular disease should note that tilapia has a less favourable omega-6 to omega-3 ratio than fatty fish such as salmon or mackerel, and should complement their diet with other omega-3-rich foods.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lean protein with low saturated fat and omega-3 fatty acids; less favourable omega-6:omega-3 ratio than fatty fish
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Steamed tilapia is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as a lean, anti-inflammatory protein source; complement with walnuts or flaxseed for stronger anti-inflammatory benefit and avoid adding soy sauce which increases sodium and may worsen RA-related swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed tilapia is a minimally processed freshwater fish dish consisting of boneless tilapia fillets cooked by steaming, a method that preserves the fish's natural moisture and nutrients without adding fat. The flesh is white, mild in flavour, and flaky in texture, making it widely accepted across age groups and dietary preferences. Tilapia is a widely farmed freshwater fish popular across Southeast Asia, including Singapore, where it is commonly served steamed with light soy sauce, ginger, and spring onion, though the plain steamed preparation contains no added condiments. Per 100 g, steamed tilapia provides 136 kcal, 22 g of high-quality protein, 5.2 g of total fat (of which approximately 1.6 g is saturated fat), and 0 g of carbohydrates, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 85 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 351 mg/100 g, supporting blood pressure management consistent with DASH dietary principles. Cholesterol is 88 mg/100 g, which is moderate and within acceptable limits for most individuals. The glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Steamed tilapia is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free, and is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease.

How many calories in steamed tilapia?
Is steamed tilapia high in protein?
Is steamed tilapia suitable for hypertension?
